# Busuanzi — self-hosted visitor counter

> A minimal, drop-in visitor counter compatible with the original
> busuanzi.ibruce.info script. Single Rust binary, SQLite-backed,
> optional pluggable web dashboard. Decoupled architecture: ship just
> the backend, or pair it with the bundled Solid admin SPA.

Landing site: __SITE_URL__/
Source code: https://github.com/AdingApkgg/bsz
LLM index: __SITE_URL__/llms.txt (this file is the expanded variant)

This file is the long-form companion to `llms.txt`, intended for IDE
assistants (Cursor, Cline, Continue, Aider) and chat agents that
benefit from the full landing-page content rendered as markdown.
Section anchors below mirror the live URL fragments.

---

## Table of contents

- [Localization](#localization)
- [Concepts](#concepts)
- [Features](#features) → __SITE_URL__/#features
- [API endpoints](#api-endpoints) → __SITE_URL__/#api

- [Quick start](#quick-start) → __SITE_URL__/#quickstart
- [Full example](#full-example) → __SITE_URL__/#example
- [Read-only (GET)](#read-only-get) → __SITE_URL__/#get
- [Silent submit (PUT)](#silent-submit-put) → __SITE_URL__/#put
- [Headers](#headers)
- [Cookies and CORS](#cookies-and-cors)
- [Stack](#stack)
- [Deploy notes](#deploy-notes)

---

## Localization

The landing site publishes three locales. Comments are shared across
all three (single thread, pinned to the origin root).

- Chinese (default): __SITE_URL__/
- English:           __SITE_URL__/en
- Japanese:          __SITE_URL__/ja

`hreflang` link tags + `og:locale:alternate` meta tags + a sitemap with
`xhtml:link` cross-references are emitted per-route. The `x-default`
URL points at the apex (zh).

---

## Concepts

A Busuanzi backend tracks three counters:

| Field     | Meaning                                      |
| --------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `site_pv` | Total page views across the entire site      |
| `site_uv` | Unique visitors across the entire site       |
| `page_pv` | Page views for the URL in `x-bsz-referer`    |

Visitor identity comes from a `busuanziId` cookie — `MD5(IP + UserAgent)`
in uppercase hex, 1-year lifetime. The scheme is wire-compatible with
the original busuanzi service, so existing client snippets keep working.

---

## Features

- Zero external runtime dependencies (pure Rust binary).
- Single binary distribution — drop the executable next to a config and
  systemd unit, done.
- SQLite persistence; no separate DB process to operate.
- In-memory cache (DashMap) for hot paths, periodic flush to SQLite.
- Drop-in compatibility with the original busuanzi `busuanziId` cookie.
- Pluggable dashboard: ship just the backend, or pair with the bundled
  Solid admin SPA. The admin SPA is decoupled and deployable to any
  static host (GitHub Pages, Cloudflare Pages, Netlify).

---

## API endpoints

All endpoints live under whichever backend origin you deploy. Substitute
`<your-backend>` with that origin — e.g. `https://bsz.example.com`.

| Method | Path    | Purpose                                                  |
| ------ | ------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| POST   | /api    | Count this hit AND return current totals (the common case) |
| GET    | /api    | Read totals WITHOUT counting (dashboards, status pages)  |
| PUT    | /api    | Count this hit, return no body (silent, fire-and-forget) |
| GET    | /ping   | Health check — returns 200 OK if the backend is live     |

Admin routes (`/api/admin/*`) only mount when the `ADMIN_TOKEN`
environment variable is set on the backend. When unset, the backend
exposes only the four public endpoints above, and the admin SPA is
effectively a static page with no live target.

---

## Response shape

All three counting endpoints (`POST /api`, `GET /api`) return:

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "site_pv": 1234,
    "site_uv": 567,
    "page_pv": 89
  }
}
```

- `success: false` indicates the request was accepted but not counted —
  typically a malformed `x-bsz-referer`.
- `PUT /api` returns `204 No Content` on success; no body to parse.

---

## Quick start

Three steps to add visitor stats to any HTML page.

### 1. Send the request

```javascript
const res = await fetch("<your-backend>/api", {
  method: "POST",
  credentials: "include",
  headers: { "x-bsz-referer": location.href },
});
```

### 2. Parse the response

```javascript
const { success, data } = await res.json();
// data.site_pv  → total site page views
// data.site_uv  → unique site visitors
// data.page_pv  → views for the current page
```

### 3. Render

```javascript
document.getElementById("pv").textContent = data.site_pv;
document.getElementById("uv").textContent = data.site_uv;
document.getElementById("ppv").textContent = data.page_pv;
```

---

## Full example

A self-contained snippet you can paste into any HTML page. Swap the URL
for your backend.

```html
<p>Site views <span id="pv">…</span> · Visitors <span id="uv">…</span></p>
<p>This page <span id="ppv">…</span></p>

<script>
(async () => {
  const res = await fetch("https://your-backend.example.com/api", {
    method: "POST",
    credentials: "include",
    headers: { "x-bsz-referer": location.href },
  });
  const { success, data } = await res.json();
  if (!success) return;
  document.getElementById("pv").textContent = data.site_pv;
  document.getElementById("uv").textContent = data.site_uv;
  document.getElementById("ppv").textContent = data.page_pv;
})();
</script>
```

---

## Read-only (GET)

Use `GET` to read counts without incrementing. Ideal for dashboards,
status pages, or any view that re-renders frequently and shouldn't
inflate its own numbers.

```javascript
const res = await fetch("<your-backend>/api", {
  headers: { "x-bsz-referer": "https://your-site.com/page" },
});
const { data } = await res.json();
```

---

## Silent submit (PUT)

Use `PUT` to record a hit without reading the response body. Useful
when you only want to track the visit and don't need to display the
counts on the same page.

```javascript
fetch("<your-backend>/api", {
  method: "PUT",
  credentials: "include",
  headers: { "x-bsz-referer": location.href },
});
```

---

## Headers

- `x-bsz-referer` (required) — full URL of the page being counted. The
  backend uses this (not the browser-sent `Referer`) to identify the
  page, so it keeps working for cross-origin embeds where the browser
  strips Referer.

---

## Cookies and CORS

- `busuanziId` — visitor identity. The backend sets it on the first
  request and reads it on subsequent ones.
- Cross-origin embeds MUST pass `credentials: "include"` to send the
  cookie back.
- The backend's CORS policy must respond with
  `Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true` AND a specific (non-`*`)
  `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` matching the embedding site.
- In production deployments where the landing and backend share an
  origin (e.g. `https://bsz.example.com` serving both static landing +
  `/api`), no CORS work is needed — the cookie is same-origin.

---

## Stack

- Rust + Axum (HTTP server)
- SQLite (durable storage, single file)
- DashMap (in-process cache for hot reads)
- Tokio (async runtime)
- Optional admin SPA: Solid + Vite + Kobalte UI, gated behind
  `ADMIN_TOKEN`. When the env var is unset, no admin routes mount on
  the backend and the SPA acts as a frontend-only template.

---

## Deploy notes

- Landing builds to a static `dist/` and works on any static host
  (GitHub Pages, Cloudflare Pages, Netlify, Vercel). 404.html and
  `_redirects` are emitted so SPA deep links (`/en`, `/ja`) survive.
- Set `VITE_SITE_URL` to bake the canonical origin into
  `sitemap.xml`, `robots.txt`, `llms.txt`, and the static `og:image`
  URL inside `index.html`.
- Set `VITE_DEMO_API` to wire the live PV/UV grid at the top of the
  landing to a reachable backend. Unset → the grid is hidden.
- Set `VITE_ARTALK_SERVER` (and optionally `VITE_ARTALK_SITE_NAME`) to
  enable the Artalk comments section at the bottom. The Artalk
  `pageKey` is pinned to `${location.origin}/`, so all three locale
  routes share one thread.
- The backend ships as a single binary plus a TOML config; see
  `backend/example/.env` and `backend/example/bsz.service` in the
  repo for a starting point.

---

project metadata.
